AusTraits is a curated, open, and FAIR data resource for Australian plant trait research. This page summarises visible indicators of use, reuse, and community engagement.

::: {.callout-note} This page was last rendered on 2026-06-01. For the latest usage counts, follow the Zenodo DOI links below. ::: ## Dataset Reach AusTraits is available through Zenodo, with multiple releases grouped under the project concept DOI: [![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.3568417.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3568417) Zenodo tracks repository usage following the [COUNTER Code of Practice for Research Data](https://www.projectcounter.org/code-practice-research-data/). The cards below use the cached Zenodo metadata stored in this repository so the site can render reliably without contacting external services each time.
17,641ViewsCached Zenodo usage metadata
12,535DownloadsCached Zenodo usage metadata
16,083Unique viewsCached Zenodo usage metadata
10,923Unique downloadsCached Zenodo usage metadata

For current citation information, see the [Google Scholar record](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=QXMISwEAAAAJ&sortby=pubdate&citation_for_view=QXMISwEAAAAJ:K3LRdlH-MEoC). Broader online attention is tracked on the [Altmetric page](https://nature.altmetric.com/details/114271024).
## Community Engagement The AusTraits team has presented the project at ecological, biodiversity informatics, vocabulary, and systematics meetings, including: - Biosystematics - ARDC Vocabulary Symposium - Atlas of Living Australia and GBIF symposiums - International Association of Vegetation Scientists - Ecological Society of America - Ecological Society of Australia - Open Traits Network AusTraits workshops or stalls have also been held at research institutions, conferences, and botanical collections, including the Australian National University, University of Melbourne, Biosystematics, Ecological Society of Australia, and Australian PlantBank. ## Reuse Pathways AusTraits impact is visible not only through downloads and citations, but also through reuse in infrastructure:

Research

Researchers use AusTraits to analyse trait variation, functional diversity, plant strategies, and ecological responses across the Australian flora.

Conservation

Trait data can support environmental assessment, threat susceptibility analysis, and prioritisation for conservation planning.

Infrastructure

AusTraits data are distributed through biodiversity platforms, including Atlas of Living Australia taxon pages.

Reusable workflows

The traits.build workflow helps other communities create harmonised trait databases with similar provenance and metadata standards.
